title: Fix Missing HostConfig field from API response

comments.body:
@Honny1 I have added the nil check and the test under the already existing compat containers/json test invocation.

I had to run the patches against 5.8.2 for tests to pass, main is currently in a broken state.

@danishprakash

It was written in the CI logs:

+ ./bin/golangci-lint run --build-tags=apparmor,seccomp,selinux
pkg/api/handlers/compat/containers.go:468:1: File is not properly formatted (gofumpt)
			ID:         l.ID(),
			
Just pushed the fixed version
I am not sure if or how this test has been passing, but the container runs a top process and simply waits for timeout  before sending a SIGABRT. This should never exit cleanly.

@danishprakash reverted. I also reverted the removal of  the majority of the ContainerCreateConfig other than the HostConfig field. pkg/api/handlers/compat/containers_create.go:50 unmarshals the containercreateconfig from the http request body. I believe this was the issue. I can't get tests running on my machine so I have to resort to just trial/error with CI.

I still think it's safe to remove `ContainerCreateConfig` as you earlier did. The failures (though I didn't check which test failed specifically) might rely on zero values for the embedded fields. Can you please revert the recent change(the addition of `ContainerCreateConfig`) and push again?
